rows.source_ref: The DIBS Method (Daily Inside Bar Setup), method by trader 'PeterCrowns', compiled/continued by jarroo in ForexFactory thread 86766 'The DIBS Method... No Free Lunch continues' (continuation of thread 56907 'No Free Lunch but all the coffee you can drink'), 2008, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/86766-the-dibs-method-no-free-lunch-continues
rows.concept: Anchor a daily reference at 06:00 GMT (00:00 GMT for JPY pairs); on an up-day (price above the open) buy the break of an hourly inside bar, on a down-day sell the break, riding intraday continuation with a stop on the far side of the IB.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic breakout: IB detection, daily-open direction filter, entry 1 pip beyond IB range +spread, initial stop at opposite IB extreme, exit half at 1:1 (FTT) then hold remainder as free trade; FX-H1 data on .DWX; bounded risk fits FTMO/DXZ.
rows.notes: Well-specified core rules; open-hour (06:00 GMT majors vs 00:00 GMT JPY) and remainder-trailing method (fixed initial stop / MA-trail) are sweepable params, not gaps. Session-structure/breakout driver, orthogonal to index/metal swing-MR. Best trades stated in first 6-9 hours after the open (London into early NY). Optional 'hot hand' relative-strength filter (trade the currency most up/down on the day) and M15/M30 variants mentioned but not the core. Forum-sourced, but detailed and rule-based (Balke EA precedent from ForexFactory). An MQ4 identifier 'DIBS_TriggerLines' was posted in-thread as reference only.
